question.content: Using the given relative-motion relation at the instant with coincident bases, compute the inertial-frame velocity vector V_A/O. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use V_A/O=V_B/O+V_A/B+omegar_A/B at the instant with coincident bases.
step_1.key_point: Set V_B/O=0 and keep i=i1, j=j1, k=k1.

step_3.content: Take r_A/B and V_A/B along +j radial direction from B to A.

step_5.content: Compute rotational term with omega=0.5*k and r=5 along +j.
step_5.key_point: omega×r gives a -i component with magnitude 2.5.

step_7.content: Add components to obtain the inertial-frame velocity vector.
step_7.key_point: Result is leftward-x component plus positive-y component.
